servers.description: Relative URL for the hosted Swagger UI

get.description: Get one player metadata record by Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

parameters.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

schema.title: Player Id
schema.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

parameters.name: player_id

body.positions: Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard
Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard

get.description: Get per-game season stat rows for a player. Optional filters: seasonType can be Regular or Playoffs; seasonId must use YYYY-YYYY format, for example 2025-2026.

parameters.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

schema.title: Player Id
schema.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

parameters.name: player_id

parameters.description: Filter by season type. Valid values: Regular or Playoffs.

schema.title: Seasontype
schema.description: Filter by season type. Valid values: Regular or Playoffs.

parameters.name: seasonType

parameters.description: Filter by NBA season in YYYY-YYYY format, for example 2025-2026.

schema.title: Seasonid
schema.description: Filter by NBA season in YYYY-YYYY format, for example 2025-2026.

parameters.name: seasonId

post.description:
List player game log rows for all available seasons with pagination support.

Usage: player_id is a Basketball Reference player ID such as jamesle01. Omit lastEvaluatedKey on the first request.

parameters.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

schema.title: Player Id
schema.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

parameters.name: player_id

firstPage.description: Use this body for the first request. Do not send lastEvaluatedKey until a response returns one.

nextPage.description: Pass the exact lastEvaluatedKey object returned by the previous response.

post.description:
List player game log rows for one NBA season with pagination support. The season_id path value is the ending season year, for example 2026 for the 2025-2026 season.

Usage: season_id is the ending season year, such as 2026 for the 2025-2026 NBA season.

parameters.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

schema.title: Player Id
schema.description: Basketball Reference player ID, for example jamesle01.

parameters.name: player_id

parameters.description: Ending NBA season year, for example 2026 for the 2025-2026 season.

schema.title: Season Id
schema.description: Ending NBA season year, for example 2026 for the 2025-2026 season.

parameters.name: season_id

firstPage.description: Use this body for the first request. Do not send lastEvaluatedKey until a response returns one.

nextPage.description: Pass the exact lastEvaluatedKey object returned by the previous response.

get.description: Get team metadata by team ID and season. The season_id path value must use YYYY-YYYY format, for example 2025-2026.

parameters.description: Basketball Reference team ID, for example LAL.

schema.title: Team Id
schema.description: Basketball Reference team ID, for example LAL.

parameters.name: team_id

parameters.description: NBA season in YYYY-YYYY format, for example 2025-2026.

schema.title: Season Id
schema.description: NBA season in YYYY-YYYY format, for example 2025-2026.

parameters.name: season_id

body.lastGameInfo: Won NBA Western Conference First Round
Won NBA Western Conference First Round

post.description:
List player metadata records with pagination support. Use this endpoint for broad player discovery.

Usage: send pageSize between 1 and 100. Omit lastEvaluatedKey on the first request.

firstPage.description: Use this body for the first request. Do not send lastEvaluatedKey until a response returns one.

nextPage.description: Pass the exact lastEvaluatedKey object returned by the previous response.

body.positions: Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard
Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard

post.description:
List player metadata records matching a lowercase request body field named firstname and optional lastname prefix.

Usage: firstname is required. lastname is optional and narrows the prefix lookup.

byFullName.summary: Search by first and last name

body.positions: Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard
Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard

post.description:
Search player metadata records by name using the Meilisearch full-text index.

Usage: query is a full-text player name search. Use offset with pageSize for search pagination.

body.positions: Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard
Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard

x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request POST 'https://basketball-head.p.rapidapi.com/players/full-text-search' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  --header 'x-rapidapi-host: basketball-head.p.rapidapi.com' \
  --header 'x-rapidapi-key: YOUR_RAPIDAPI_KEY' \
  --data '{"query":"lebron james","pageSize":10,"offset":0}'

post.description:
List player metadata records matching a last name prefix with pagination support.

Usage: prefix is a case-insensitive last-name prefix.

body.positions: Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard
Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard
Small Forward, Power Forward, Point Guard

body.lastGameInfo: Won NBA Western Conference First Round

pageSize.title: Pagesize
pageSize.description: Number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100.

lastEvaluatedKey.title: Lastevaluatedkey
lastEvaluatedKey.description: Omit on the first request. For the next page, pass the exact lastEvaluatedKey object returned by the previous response.

pageSize.description: Number of records to return. Must be between 1 and 100.

lastEvaluatedKey.title: Lastevaluatedkey
lastEvaluatedKey.description: Omit on the first request. For the next page, pass the exact lastEvaluatedKey object returned by the previous response.

firstname.title: Firstname
firstname.description: First name prefix to search. Values are case-insensitive.

lastname.title: Lastname
lastname.description: Optional last name prefix filter. Use an empty string or omit it to search by first name only.
